title: Teaching
text:
Courses · Seminars · Summer Schools
Over twenty years of teaching statistics, machine learning, and data science across bachelor’s, master’s, and doctoral programmes — at the University of Naples Federico II and in international summer schools.

title: Teaching
text:
Courses Taught

Statistics for Social Sciences — Undergraduate course, Bachelor in Tourism Sciences
Sampling Surveys — Undergraduate course, Bachelor in Economics
Statistical Methods for Social Research — Graduate level, Master in Economics, Curriculum Data Scientist for Economics
Lab in Machine Learning and Text Mining with R — Graduate level, Master in Economics, Curriculum Data Scientist for Economics
Machine Learning Methods — Ph.D. courses
Bibliometric Analysis using R — Ph.D. courses and International Summer Schools

title: Teaching
text:
Seminars and Training Activities
Over the last few years I have delivered more than 60 invited seminars and training sessions on bibliometric methods, machine learning, and data science for social research. These lectures have been held in many Italian doctoral schools — including the Universities of Bergamo, Brescia, Calabria, Genoa, Milan Polytechnic, Naples, Pisa, Rome, and Salento — as well as at international institutions such as the Universities of Bodø (Norway), Edinburgh, Granada, London, Melbourne, and Paris.
I have also been a lecturer at the European Summer School for Scientometrics (ESSS), the Summer School in Science Mapping (SSSM), and the Social Statistics Summer School in Monteriggioni.

title: Teaching
text:
Conferences and Workshops
Active participant in numerous national and international conferences — DSSR, SDS, ASA, COMPSTAT, IFCS, CLADAG, JADT — where I have presented research contributions related to bibliometric analysis, classification methods, and methodological innovation in the social sciences.
Member of the scientific or organising committees of major conferences, including JADT 2022 and several editions of the Italian Statistics Society (SIS) meetings.

Course materials & updates for students
Full course materials, detailed teaching information, and live updates for students are available on my official university webpage:
docenti.unina.it/massimo.aria

title: Scientific Software
text:
Catalogue of open-source tools

Open-source scientific software I have authored or co-authored. Most packages are available on CRAN and GitHub.



Bibliometrix


R package · CRAN · 1.35M+ installations

A comprehensive toolbox for science mapping and bibliometric analysis. Supports the complete workflow: data import, descriptive analysis, network and conceptual mapping, and visualisation. → bibliometrix.org



Biblioshiny


Shiny web-app · Interactive front-end for Bibliometrix

A user-friendly Shiny interface for Bibliometrix, enabling researchers to perform advanced bibliometric and science-mapping analyses without writing code.



TALL — Text Analysis for All


R/Shiny · Text mining · NLP

A general-purpose application for text-data analysis, supporting topic modelling, frequency analysis, clustering, and content classification. → github.com/massimoaria/tall



E2Tree


R package · Explainable ML

An explainable machine-learning approach based on ensemble trees. Generates interpretable models from complex data, with visual exploration of decision rules. → github.com/massimoaria/e2tree



DimensionsR


R package · API client

Programmatic access to the Dimensions API — publications, grants, clinical trials, and patents. → github.com/massimoaria/dimensionsR



PubmedR


R package · API client · Medical literature

An R interface to the PubMed API for searching, downloading, and analysing biomedical literature metadata — useful for systematic reviews and medical informatics. → github.com/massimoaria/pubmedR



contentanalysis


R package · Scientific PDF analysis

Tools for extracting and analysing scientific content from PDFs, including citation extraction and reference matching. → github.com/massimoaria/contentanalysis



openalexR


R package · ROpenSci · API client

An R client for the OpenAlex API, providing access to open bibliographic metadata for scholarly publications, authors, institutions, and citations. → docs.ropensci.org/openalexR



PLS-SEM Matlab Toolbox


Matlab / Octave · Structural equation modelling

A Matlab / Octave package for Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Models. → github.com/massimoaria/PLS-SEM

title: Curriculum Vitae
text:
Scientific Results & Impact
Author of over 200 scientific articles published in international journals, of which 38 rated “A” (top tier) by the Italian Agency of University Research Evaluation (ANVUR).
Total citations: 26,100 · H-Index: 43 · Last 5 years: 23,200 citations, H-Index 38 (Google Scholar).
The 2017 paper “bibliometrix: An R-tool for comprehensive science mapping analysis” (Journal of Informetrics) is the most-cited article ever published in the journal (Scopus: 10,300+; Google Scholar: 19,100+ citations) and a top-downloaded article.

title: Curriculum Vitae
text:
Research Interests
My research activities span a wide range of areas in computational and applied statistics, with a strong focus on methodological development and empirical applications. In particular:

Bibliometrics & Science Mapping — Statistical techniques for analysing publication patterns, impact assessment, and systematic literature reviews
Text Mining and NLP — Corpus analysis of scientific literature and textual datasets
Machine Learning — Tree-based models, ensemble methods, neural networks, clustering, and factorial methods
Missing Data Imputation — Recursive, incremental algorithms for imputing missing values in qualitative and quantitative data
Computational Statistics — Development of estimation algorithms and numerical procedures
Statistical Survey Design — Design and execution of socio-economic surveys and customer-satisfaction studies

title: Massimo Aria
text:
Massimo Aria





Vol. XXIV · Personal Pages · MMXXVI


Massimo Aria


Professor of Statistics for Social Sciences — University of Naples Federico II



Fields


Bibliometrics · Science mapping · Machine learning · Text mining


Department


Economics and Statistics


Spin-off


K-Synth srl, co-founder


Software


bibliometrix · biblioshiny · TALL · openalexR






200+ Peer-reviewed articles


29,000+ Google Scholar citations


43 H-index


1.47M+ bibliometrix installations


The long form

Welcome. I am a full professor of Statistics for Social Sciences at the Department of Economics and Statistics of the University of Naples Federico II. My research lies at the intersection of bibliometrics, science mapping, text mining, statistical modelling, and machine learning.

I am the author of over two hundred scientific articles and the creator of several open-source R packages, including the widely used bibliometrix — with more than 1.47M installations worldwide — and TALL, an interactive environment for text analysis. I am co-founder of K-Synth, an academic spin-off dedicated to transforming data into actionable knowledge through innovative statistical and computational methods.
Over the years I have led and contributed to numerous national and international research programmes — PRIN, PNRR, EU Framework, Horizon 2020 — and I actively support open science and reproducible research.
Beyond the academy, I am happily married to Adelaide and the proud father of Vittoria and Lorenzo — they keep life joyful, unpredictable, and deeply inspiring.

Navigate this site

Curriculum Vitae — positions, projects, editorial activities
Publications — selected journal articles and monographs
Software — R packages and scientific tools
Research Statistics — live metrics from Google Scholar and CRAN
Projects · Teaching · Photos · Contacts

title: Research Projects
text:
Research Projects · Principal Investigations
Selected ongoing and past research projects — European frameworks, national PRIN / PNRR programmes, and regional initiatives.

title: Research Projects
text:
Ongoing Projects


PRIN 2022 · MIUR · Principal Investigator (2023 – 2025)


SciK-Health — The value of scientific production for patient care in Academic Health Science Centres

Project website: massimoaria.github.io/scikhealth
This research project aims to map the full spectrum of scientific output — beyond academic publications — of Italian public Academic Health Centers (AHCs) from 2000 onwards. Its purpose is twofold: to provide citizens and healthcare professionals with a science-based orientation tool, and to offer academic managers, funders, and policy-makers an evidence-based decision-making instrument.
The study involves the collection and integration of diverse research outputs — publications, patents, clinical trials, grants, altmetrics — drawn from both traditional and next-generation databases. Using science mapping and longitudinal analysis, the project reconstructs the conceptual and social structures of knowledge produced by AHCs, resulting in the creation of a Digital Conceptual and Social Atlas. A dedicated web application, SciK-Health, will disseminate these findings to the public, serving as a health-literacy and decision-support tool. A restricted-access tool will provide institutional stakeholders with a synthetic indicator — R-daisy — summarising each hospital’s research expertise. The project concludes with a validation phase involving academic hospitals and regional health agencies, ensuring the robustness and applicability of its outputs.



PRIN 2022 PNRR · Local Scientific Coordinator (2023 – 2025)


The impact of scientific production on healthcare outcomes in Italian Academic Health Science Centres

Academic Health Science Centres (AHSCs) share three strategic missions: training healthcare professionals, developing innovative technologies and digital solutions, and delivering high-quality, data-driven care. Balancing these missions often generates strategic ambiguity and organisational tension. While existing studies have occasionally examined how teaching influences patient care, little is known about the effects of research on clinical outcomes.
This project investigates whether and how academic research performance influences patient-care quality in Italian public AHSCs. It will begin by constructing a longitudinal database of scientometric and bibliometric metadata, covering publications, patents, clinical trials, grants, and altmetrics from 2000 to the present.
Following data collection we will measure productivity, growth, impact, and novelty, and construct a composite weighted indicator to evaluate the scientific production of each AHSC. Finally, using econometric models, the project will analyse how research activity correlates with key dimensions of healthcare performance — 30-day mortality, hospital readmission rates, patient mobility, waiting lists, and case-mix complexity.
The research aims to answer three central questions: does research performance in AHSCs lead to improved health outcomes? Is research activity a driver of hospital case-mix? And finally, does academic research performance influence patient choices regarding healthcare providers?

title: Research Projects
text:
Past Projects
Two decades of collaborative research
Over the past two decades I have contributed to and coordinated a wide range of research projects at the European, national, and regional levels.

European Level
Senior researcher in several EU-funded initiatives, including the Horizon 2020 project MAGIC — Moving Towards Adaptive Governance in Complexity, which addressed nexus-security challenges. Within the 7th Framework Programme I participated in EUROC — European Robotics Challenges and RoDyMan — Robotic Dynamic Manipulation, focusing on advanced robotics. Earlier, I contributed to iWeb-Care, a project on fraud detection in healthcare e-government services, and INSPECTOR, which developed distributed systems for data validation — both funded under the 6th Framework Programme. I also acted as Scientific Coordinator for the transnational cooperation initiative “Network for Regional Development and Entrepreneurship” under the EQUAL Phase 2 Programme, and as Scientific Committee member in the related SPIDER project on territorial information management for social enterprises.


National Level
Involvement in multiple PRIN (Projects of National Interest) and FIRB initiatives. These include my role as Principal Investigator for the current PRIN 2022 project SciK-Health and as Local Scientific Coordinator for the PRIN 2022 PNRR project on the impact of scientific production in Academic Health Science Centres. I also contribute to PNRR-funded initiatives such as the Digital Health Ecosystem, where I coordinate Work Package 2, and the National Centre for Sustainable Mobility, where I serve as a member of Spoke 10.
In previous years I was senior researcher for PRIN projects on rationality and attention in children, tree-based methods for classification and regression, and knowledge discovery in decision-making. I also collaborated in the FIRB project on Supply Chain Management led by the University of Salerno.


Regional & University-Funded Projects
Studies on health inequalities, childhood-care services in southern Italy, women’s re-employment post-maternity, and regional tourism monitoring systems. I coordinated projects including SOCIAL-NET (aging and social exclusion), MoniTur (cultural-tourism data analysis), and the data-strategy component of the City of Naples Strategic Plan. I further contributed as senior researcher to ICT-focused programmes co-financed by the Campania and Puglia Regions — VIRNET, ANGELO, PROTEO, PORTUR, TRITONE — as well as to the Regional Competence Centre on Transport focused on demand modelling through statistical methods.

title: Short Bio
text:
Profile · Massimo Aria
Full Professor in Statistics for Social Sciences at the Department of Economics and Statistics of the University of Naples Federico II. Ph.D. in Computational Statistics.
My main research fields include bibliometrics, science mapping, systematic literature reviews, statistical survey methodology, machine learning, and text mining. I have led numerous statistical surveys and data-science projects related to economic, social, ethological, and healthcare phenomena.

title: Short Bio
text:
Scientific Activities
I have authored more than 200 peer-reviewed scientific articles, of which 38 have been classified as “A” (top tier) in scientific area 13 (Economics and Statistics) by ANVUR (Italian Agency of University Research Evaluation).
I am the co-founder of the K-Synth Academic Spin-off, a science-centric information and intelligence start-up focused on the extraction and analysis of knowledge from large volumes of heterogeneous data through quantitative, statistical, and data-science methods.
I am the Scientific Coordinator of the Summer School in Science Mapping (SSSM) and a faculty member of the European Summer School of Scientometrics (ESSS).

title: Short Bio
text:
Academic and Institutional Roles

Board member of the Naples Ph.D. Program in Economics
Board member for over 20 years of the Ph.D. programs in Computational Statistics and Social Sciences and Statistics
Since 2019, board member of the Master’s degree in Data Science and in Economics (curriculum: Data Scientist for Economics)
Since 2010, I have coordinated — together with Prof. C. Cuccurullo — the research group in bibliometrics and science mapping at the University of Naples Federico II and the University of Campania L. Vanvitelli (www.bibliometrix.org)
I have coordinated or participated in 5 European projects, 3 PNRR projects, 5 PRIN projects, 1 FIRB project, and 11 national or regional projects
Since 2007, scientific committee member of the Statistical Algorithms and Software Specialized Team, ERCIM Working Group on Computational and Methodological Statistics
Currently responsible for the Research Quality Assessment (VQR) of the Department of Economics and Statistics

title: Short Bio
text:
Scientific Societies
Senior member of the following scientific societies:

Società Italiana di Statistica (SIS)
Associazione Italiana Studi di Popolazione (AISP)
Associazione di Statistica Applicata (ASA)
International Statistical Institute (ISI)
International Association for Statistical Computing (IASC)

title: Short Bio
text:
Scientific Results and Awards
To date, my publications have received over 26,100 citations, and my H-index is 43. In the last five years alone, my publications have been cited more than 23,200 times, with an H-index of 38 (source: Google Scholar).
My article “bibliometrix: An R-tool for comprehensive science mapping analysis” (2017, Journal of Informetrics, IF 5.107) is:

The most-cited article ever in the journal (Scopus: 10,300+ citations; Google Scholar: 19,100+)
One of the top-downloaded articles on ScienceDirect (source)

title: Short Bio
section: Social Impact of My Research Activities
text:
Social Impact of My Research Activities
Bibliometrix and its Shiny web-app Biblioshiny are recognised as the most comprehensive and user-friendly software for science mapping. In a widely cited review of bibliometric tools, Moral-Muñoz et al. (2020) wrote:

At this moment, maybe Bibliometrix and its Shiny platform contain the more extensive set of techniques implemented, and together with the easiness of its interface, could be a great software for practitioners.

Today, Bibliometrix is one of the most used science-mapping tools worldwide, with:

1,350,000+ installations
A large international community of researchers actively using and promoting it
Over 200 YouTube tutorials in multiple languages

In the past four years I have delivered over 60 seminars in Italian doctoral schools (Universities of Bergamo, Brescia, Calabria, Genoa, Polytechnic of Milan, Naples, Pisa, Rome, Salento, among others) and international ones (Universities of Bodø — Norway, Edinburgh, Granada, London, Melbourne, Paris).
